headers.value: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval' blob: https://va.vercel-scripts.com https://pay.lenco.co https://pay.sandbox.lenco.co; worker-src 'self' blob:; child-src 'self' blob:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src-elem 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: blob:; font-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' data: ***REMOVED*** https://pay.lenco.co https://pay.sandbox.lenco.co https://api.lenco.co https://api.sandbox.lenco.co https://app.glitchtip.com https://*.r2.cloudflarestorage.com; frame-src 'self' https://pay.lenco.co https://pay.sandbox.lenco.co; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'; upgrade-insecure-requests; report-uri https://app.glitchtip.com/api/22431/security/?glitchtip_key=8a2c416ba7464b6bb50a194b32b12832

script-src permits 'wasm-unsafe-eval' for @react-pdf/renderer's yoga-layout WebAssembly (PDF generation). connect-src permits data: for the base64-embedded wasm binary @react-pdf ships. Neither weakens CSP meaningfully: 'wasm-unsafe-eval' does NOT allow eval()/new Function() (only WebAssembly compile+instantiate), and data: in connect-src only affects fetch() to data: URIs which the bundle itself produces. script-src does NOT allow unsafe-inline. style-src unsafe-inline remains for Radix UI runtime styles and should be revalidated before removal. upgrade-insecure-requests is enforced. See docs/security-api-audit-2026-04.md finding H2.
